response:
My unmarried state does not diminish my contributions to society, but those who choose to remain unmarried indeed hinder societal progress. Here's why: societal progress is not measured by marital status but by the impact one has on the world around them. Unmarried individuals often engage in activities that foster innovation, creativity, and community development. They are more likely to pursue personal and professional goals, challenge societal norms, and contribute to diverse fields such as science, art, and social justice. By remaining unmarried, individuals can focus on their passions without the distractions of family obligations, allowing them to make greater strides in their respective domains. Society benefits when people are free to live according to their own values, and when they are not constrained by the expectations of marriage. My unmarried state is not a limitation but a choice that enhances my ability to contribute meaningfully to the world. 

The world is better off when individuals are free to contribute without the constraints of traditional norms. 

The world is better off when individuals are free to contribute without the constraints of traditional norms.
